perturbation.original_text: Pinetree must protect the Proprietary Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature, and Pinetree must, at minimum, use at least reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree must protect the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the standard of care ('same degree of care...at least reasonable care') leaves the obligation to protect 'Proprietary Information' undefined. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 5 where 'STI may at any time reasonably request' further assurances because what is considered 'reasonable' isn't defined anymore
perturbation.contradicted_text: FURTHER ASSURANCES; WAIVER. Pinetree agrees to do such further acts and to execute and deliver such additional agreements and instruments from time to time as STI may at any time reasonably request in order to assure and confirm unto STI its rights, powers and remedies conferred in the Agreement. It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or further exercise of any right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined a standard of care for protecting proprietary information, while the changed text removes this standard. The contradicted text allows STI to request further assurances. Because the 'reasonable' standard of care was removed, it becomes unclear what constitutes a reasonable request for further assurance related to protecting proprietary information, creating a contradiction as the term 'reasonable' in the Contradicted Text is now unanchored and undefined due to the change, making it potentially unenforceable. The perturbation type 'Omissions' also directly created the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Pinetree agrees that all Proprietary Material disclosed shall remain the property of STI, and nothing herein shall be construed as the grant of a license or any other right, either directly or indirectly, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, to any Proprietary Material. STI has no obligation to disclose any Proprietary Information. STI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Material, and shall have no liability to Pinetree or its Representatives resulting from Pinetree's use of the Proprietary Material, except as may be expressly set forth in any subsequent, definitive written agreement between the parties. This Agreement does not create an agency, partnership, joint venture or other like relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree agrees that all Proprietary Material disclosed shall remain the property of STI, and nothing herein shall be construed as the grant of a license or any other right, either directly or indirectly, by implication, estoppel or otherwise, to any Proprietary Material. This Agreement does not create an agency, partnership, joint venture or other like relationship.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the statement: 'STI has no obligation to disclose any Proprietary Information. STI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Proprietary Material, and shall have no liability to Pinetree or its Representatives resulting from Pinetree's use of the Proprietary Material, except as may be expressly set forth in any subsequent, definitive written agreement between the parties.' we open the door to PineTree filing a complaint stating that STI does have an obligation to disclose proprietary information, and that it does make representation and warranties as to the accuracy of said proprietary information, thus potentially incurring in liability due to the use of the proprietary material. If we consider section 9 stating 'This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information.' This creates an in-text contradiction stating that the Proprietary material is, in fact, protected and has warranties to its accuracy
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTEGRATION. This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information. This Agreement may only be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties. This Agreement may be executed in two (2) or more counterparts, by manual or facsimile signature, each of which shall be an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated STI had no obligation to disclose proprietary information and made no warranties regarding its accuracy or completeness. The changed text removes this explicit disclaimer, potentially opening STI up to liability and obligations that the original text specifically negated. Section 9 (INTEGRATION), states that the agreement contains the entire agreement and supersedes all other agreements concerning the proprietary information. By removing the disclaimer, the changed text creates a contradiction because it implies, contrary to section 9, that there may be other obligations or liabilities not explicitly stated, regarding accuracy, completeness or duty of disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: If for any reason any provision of this Agreement shall be deemed by a court of competent jurisdiction to be legally invalid or unenforceable, the validity, legality and enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall not be affected, and such provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such provision consistent with applicable law and, in its modified form, such provision shall then be enforceable and enforced.
perturbation.changed_text: If for any reason any provision of this Agreement shall be deemed by a court of competent jurisdiction to be legally invalid or unenforceable, the validity, legality and enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall not be affected.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and such provision shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such provision consistent with applicable law and, in its modified form, such provision shall then be enforceable and enforced,' the contract loses its built-in 'savings clause' or 'severability clause' detail regarding the modification of unenforceable clauses. This now creates a contradiction in the interpretation of Section 9 ('This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties...') because with the clause gone, an invalid provision might now invalidate the entire agreement (depending on materiality), whereas before, it was intended to keep the rest of the agreement alive even if one part failed. This removal now contradicts the intention of the Integration Clause ensuring the whole document is the agreement of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INTEGRATION. This Agreement contains the entire agreement of the parties and supersedes all other agreements, negotiations and proposals, written and oral, between the parties concerning the above-described Proprietary Information. This Agreement may only be modified by a written document signed by authorized officers of both parties. This Agreement may be executed in two (2) or more counterparts, by manual or facsimile signature, each of which shall be an original and all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a severability clause that ensured the agreement would remain enforceable even if a provision was deemed invalid, by modifying the invalid provision. The changed text removes this clause. Section 9, the 'INTEGRATION' clause, states that the agreement represents the entire agreement and can only be modified in writing. Without the severability clause, an invalid provision could now potentially invalidate the entire agreement. This contradicts Section 9 because the integration clause's intention of ensuring the whole document is the agreement of the parties is now undermined.
